channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: David Zaslav and the tyranny of incentives
item.description: The Warner CEO’s payout is a cautionary tale

item.title: Pakistan Keeps Pushing for Peace
item.description: A first round of U.S.-Iran talks didn’t lead to a deal, but Islamabad wants to stay at the table.

item.title: Magyar’s Big Plans for Budapest
item.description: Hungary’s incoming leader aims to tackle corruption, the previous administration’s influence, and dependence on Russia.

item.title: How to end the war in Iran
item.description: America and Iran must resume their efforts to strike a deal, even though it is sure to be an imperfect one

item.title: How natural selection really shaped humanity
item.description: Adaptation in response to evolutionary pressure is surprisingly common

item.title: Orban-proofing the EU
item.description: How the leader of a small, poor country held such sway over the bloc

item.title: Zimbabwe’s bizarre economic boom
item.description: A gold rush is combined with a power grab

item.title: How AI hackers will shake up cyber-security
item.description: The technology could eventually favour the defenders—but expect a bumpy ride

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s Pentagon is a lethality-maxxing wasps’ nest
item.description: America’s armed forces are supremely capable and roiled by infighting

item.title: Sudan’s Forgotten War Enters Its Fourth Year
item.description: The world’s worst humanitarian crisis threatens to engulf the wider region.

item.title: Why Xi Is Kneecapping His Own Top Men
item.description: Targeting the inner circle of leadership shows no one is safe.

item.title: How Strategic Investment Is Redefining Economic Statecraft
item.description: In a global economy defined by intertwined supply chains and cross-border capital flows, economic security cannot rely solely on national investment. Fund-of-funds vehicles can help governments bolster resilience and advance strategic objectives while protecting sensitive technologies and limiting adversarial influence.

item.title: Why Anthropic and OpenAI are locking up their latest models
item.description: Allowing only a privileged few companies to use them may make commercial sense

item.title: India Mulls Deployment of Crocodiles and Snakes at Border with Bangladesh
item.description: The plan was discussed even as Indian and Bangladeshi diplomats are working to reset bilateral relations.

item.title: Bangladesh at the Crossroads: Renewables or Imported Fuel Chaos
item.description: The energy crisis is hitting Bangladesh’s 177 million people hard. Experts have been urging the government to end dependence on imported coal and gas in favor of reliable renewables.

item.title: The Next UN Secretary-General Must Be a Woman
item.description: As United Nations member states prepare to elect a new secretary-general, they should break with precedent and choose a woman. This would not be a corrective gesture or a form of symbolic representation, but rather a strategic acknowledgment that leadership selection must reflect the realities of contemporary governance.

item.title: The Christian Roots of Kimilsungism
item.description: Insights from Jonathan Cheng.

item.title: Why the US Needs the PROSPER in the Pacific Act
item.description: “If the United States is serious about a stable and resilient Indo-Pacific, it must act to strengthen its economic relationships” with the Pacific Islands, argues U.S. Rep. Ed Case.

item.title: War will drain the Gulf’s $6trn treasure chest
item.description: The conflict complicates life for the custodians of Middle Eastern oil fortunes

item.title: South Korea’s Harder Line on Israel Amid Energy Shock
item.description: It’s not just about Israel: Seoul is signaling political confidence and a growing readiness to shape its own external posture separately from the United States.

item.title: Officer Charged in 2024 Suicide of Young Kazakh Conscript
item.description: The charging of the officer comes amid increased scrutiny of the deaths of Kazakh soldiers, often quite young, during peacetime.

item.title: Amid the Hormuz Crisis, Indonesia Rewrites Its Energy Playbook
item.description: Through summit diplomacy and state visits, Prabowo is widening Indonesia’s options across oil, gas, minerals, industrial inputs, and energy transition technologies.

item.title: Hungary’s New Leader Must Avoid Joe Biden’s Mistakes
item.description: If Hungary can become a model for illiberal movements elsewhere to emulate, it can also become a sandbox for experimenting with democratic renewal. America and others suffering democratic backsliding can offer lessons for Péter Magyar's new government, whose successes or failures, in turn, will generate new lessons.

item.title: Why Israel’s Post-Oct. 7 Security Doctrine Has Failed
item.description: From Gaza to Lebanon to Iran, Netanyahu has sought to end threats rather than manage them.

item.title: China’s $4.5 Billion Headache: The Niger-Benin Pipeline and the Limits of Non-Interference
item.description: The question is no longer whether Beijing is meddling in African affairs, but how much longer it can pretend that it isn’t.

item.title: Investigation: Is Shakhmurat Mutalip Kazakhstan’s New Chosen One?
item.description: The 35-year-old is building an empire spanning railways, energy and mining. Most Kazakhs have never heard of him.

item.title: Analysing Africa newsletter: A special edition answering your questions
item.description: John McDermott, our Chief Africa correspondent, rummages through the postbag

item.title: Middle East Dispatch newsletter: Decades under the smoke trail
item.description: Anshel Pfeffer, our Jerusalem correspondent, visits Kiryat Shmona, by the Israeli-Lebanese border

item.title: Putting Africa’s Savings to Work
item.description: Africa’s pension funds and financial institutions hold hundreds of billions of dollars in long-term savings, yet much of that capital flows into sovereign bonds because few scalable alternatives exist. The task now is to build the architecture that channels those savings into productive investments.

item.title: Prabowo’s Russian Roulette
item.description: The Indonesian president visits Moscow—and signs a U.S. defense agreement.

item.title: Why Did China Buy Up the World’s Ports?
item.description: Beijing is less focused on acquiring sovereign control, more so in assuring its own strategic security.

item.title: Cheap Drones Complicate the Gulf’s AI Boom
item.description: Protection is becoming inseparable from access.

item.title: The Tech High Ground
item.description: What it will take to gain the advantage over China.

item.title: How Orbán Defeated Himself
item.description: Lessons from Hungary for pro-democracy movements worldwide.

item.title: For Iran, Hormuz Is More a Weakness Than a Weapon
item.description: A U.S. blockade threatens the regime's grip.
